title: pergularia daemia subsp. barbata
intro: Pergularia daemia subsp. barbata, also known as 'Dhatura' or 'Aak', is a species of poisonous plant belonging to the Solanaceae family, commonly found in the deserts of India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits associated with Pergularia daemia subsp. barbata include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and act as a sedative. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and powders, although its toxic nature requires careful handling and dosing.
